resolution.note: Legacy apparent-BE marker 497 is the SAME Auger line as NIST KE 994.4 eV, but under a different display convention: the legacy marker implies hv-wf ≈ 1491.4 eV (legacy 497 + KE 994.4), vs the app's hv-wf = 1482.1 (which would put the apparent BE at 487.7). Store as KINETIC energy (corroborated by both passes); the app computes the apparent-BE marker from KE via hv-KE-wf, so the convention mismatch is handled at display time.

Legacy apparent-BE marker 306 is the SAME Auger line as NIST KE 1185.65 eV, but under a different display convention: the legacy marker implies hv-wf ≈ 1491.7 eV (legacy 306 + KE 1185.65), vs the app's hv-wf = 1482.1 (which would put the apparent BE at 296.4). Store as KINETIC energy (corroborated by both passes); the app computes the apparent-BE marker from KE via hv-KE-wf, so the convention mismatch is handled at display time.

Legacy marker 133 was oxide/compound-positioned. Elemental (metal) nominal 130.15 eV corroborated by both extraction passes; expected region widened to [129.4, 134.5] to cover the legacy oxidation state. Same pattern as the curated U 4f.
